mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next month when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis but SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered observations or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in retail sales shows demand momentum that supports corporate revenue expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS accelerates above its prior 3-month trend.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average yet SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in at least 60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which should depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when UNRATE increases by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp in 3 months but SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal's hit rate falls below baseline by 10 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stronger payroll growth indicates broad real-sector expansion, which should support earnings revisions and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition INDPRO and PAYEMS both trend higher for 2 months but SPX 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases or the interaction term has no incremental predictive power versus each series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy rate relative to inflation indicates restrictive real rates that compress equity multiples, so when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPI inflation is not falling, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL not declining over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the restrictive-rate plus sticky-inflation condition is met but SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances or the joint signal underperforms FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts reflects easing financial conditions and stronger cyclically sensitive demand, which should lift equity returns over the following week as investors price a softer growth slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rises more than 5% month-over-month but SPX forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of such events or the average return is not above baseline by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is stable or falling while payrolls continue to grow, labor income expectations improve and reduce recession pricing, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE is flat-to-down and PAYEMS is rising but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of cases or the combined signal has no edge over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production weakens while unemployment rises, the market should price an approaching earnings slowdown and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 straight months and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the simultaneous INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise occurs but SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances or the interaction does not improve hit rate versus INDPRO alone.
